channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Find these 6 Easter eggs on your iPhone or Mac
item.description: If real Easter eggs aren’t your thing this weekend, you may find hunting for digital ones more enjoyable. And there are some cool ones to find at your fingertips, provided you have an iPhone or Mac.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: A TikToker sues Roblox for using her viral Charli XCX dance without permission
item.description: If you thought you’d heard the last of the viral “Apple” dance, think again. The TikToker behind it is now suing Roblox over its unauthorized use.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: You freak out when battery life hits 38%, but here's how to extend it and calm the heck down
item.description: A new report puts our smartphone battery concerns in perspective and we have some tips to make it through the day.
item.dc:creator: lance.ulanoff@futurenet.com (Lance Ulanoff), TechRadar

item.title: Google "could face breakup" after being found guilty of having illegal ad tech monopolies
item.description: Google has been found guilty of antitrust law violation with regards to its ads market monopolization – and the consequences could be major.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: JetBrains IDEs now include AI tools by subscription
item.description: JetBrains AI tools, including the improved AI Assistant tool and coding agent dubbed Junie, are now available with a single subscription and with a free tier for JetBrains IDEs.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Learning how to measure genAI’s impact
item.description: Top picks for generative AI readers on InfoWorld AI will require more software developers, not fewer Data show AI assistants make it significantly faster and easier to develop software, spurring companies to tackle more software projects—and (surprise!) hire more developers.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Moto Razr Plus 2025 Leaks: Upgrades and Details Revealed
item.description: Motorola Razr Plus 2025 specs leaked, upgrades include Snapdragon 8 Elite processor, 16GB RAM, and a 4,700 mAh battery.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: New Jersey is suing Discord for allegedly violating child safety laws
item.description: New Jersey filed a lawsuit against Discord on Thursday, alleging that the social platform recklessly exposed children to “harassment, abuse, and sexual exploitation by predators who lurk on the platform.” The move makes it the first state to sue Discord.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Instagram launches ‘Blend’ to share personalized Reels with friends
item.description: When it comes to sharing Instagram Reels with friends, the process of three taps to get a Reel from A to B can feel surprisingly tedious. Now, Instagram has addressed that issue with its latest feature: Instagram Blend.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Nvidia watches its Trump overtures come to naught
item.description: Welcome to AI Decoded, Fast Company’s weekly newsletter that breaks down the most important news in the world of AI. You can sign up to receive this newsletter every week here.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Google's digital ad network declared an illegal monopoly, joining its search engine in penalty box
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google has been branded an abusive monopolist by a federal judge for the second time in less than a year, this time for illegally exploiting some of its online marketing technology to boost the profits fueling an internet empire currently worth $1.8 trillion.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: IBM X-Force: Stealthy attacks on the rise, toolkits targeting AI emerge
item.description: Cybercriminals are adopting increasingly stealthy tactics for breaking into networks, while attacks targeting specific AI technologies are an emerging threat.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Broadcom (quietly) brings back free VMware ESXi hypervisor
item.description: With little notice or fanfare, Broadcom is offering a free version of the VMware ESXi hypervisor for download. VMware vSphere Hypervisor version 8 can be downloaded from Broadcom’s support portal for free, but registration is required.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: San Jose mayor Mahan, local congressman Liccardo lend support as Waymo applies to bring robotaxis to the city
item.description: Waymo initially faced a rocky road in bringing its pioneering technology to Bay Area streets and highways.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Baron, The Mercury News

item.title: CoreWeave offers cloud-based Grace Blackwell GPUs for AI training
item.description: Cloud services provider CoreWeave has announced it is offering Nvidia’s GB200 NVL72 systems, otherwise known as “Grace Blackwell,” to customers looking to do intensive AI training.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Apple has removed yet another popular VPN app from its Russian App Store
item.description: NORSE Labs DVPN adds to the over 100 VPN apps currently unavailable in Russia's Apple App Store. Here's all you need to know.
item.dc:creator: chiara.castro@futurenet.com (Chiara Castro), TechRadar

item.title: Many Americans share mistrust of artificial intelligence: Survey
item.description: Three-quarters of Americans say they trust information generated by artificial intelligence (AI) only sometimes or hardly ever, according to a recent survey.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: 7 simple ways to protect your credit cards while traveling
item.description: Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson shares several easy ways to keep your credit cards safe from digital thieves while you're traveling this summer.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: GE Vernova’s CEO on thriving through tariffs and supply chain shifts
item.description: Amid tariff whiplash and the rejuggling of global trade, GE Vernova’s CEO Scott Strazik is finding a way to stay “relentlessly optimistic.
item.dc:creator: Robert Safian, Fast Company

item.title: 10 most used gen AI tools in the enterprise
item.description: Generative AI has entered the enterprise in a big way since OpenAI first launched ChatGPT in 2022. According to Precedence Research, the global gen AI market was over $25 billion in 2024 and is forecast to reach a staggering $803 billion by 2033.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Trump's potential semiconductor tariffs spook tech industry
item.description: President Trump’s potential tariffs on semiconductors are stoking alarm within the technology sector as companies brace for ripple effects across the industry and its competitive standing on a global stage.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Trump, Musk, RFK Jr., Lutnick make Time's 'most influential' list
item.description: Time magazine has named President Trump and five members of his administration to its list of the 100 Most Influential People of 2025, highlighting their significant impact on immigration, foreign policy, international trade, and the federal workforce.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, The Hill

item.title: Tech shares fall after Nvidia says new US controls on exports of AI chip will cost it $5.5 billion
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Shares in computer chipmakers slumped Wednesday after Nvidia said tighter U.S. government controls on exports of computer chips used for artificial intelligence will cost it an extra $5.5 billion.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Hackers steal 1.6 million patient records in major healthcare breach
item.description: The CyberGuy explains why healthcare organizations are vulnerable to hackers after there have been a half dozen data breaches this year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Analysts: Go slow on M365, Google Workspace ‘agent-ish’ AI rollouts
item.description: The last few months have seen an explosion of new agentic-like AI tools that can automate workflows within productivity applications, but analysts are recommending that enterprises carefully assess deployment risks before adopting them.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: 6 languages you can deploy to WebAssembly right now
item.description: WebAssembly, or Wasm, gives developers a way to create programs that run at near-native speed in the browser or anywhere else you can deploy the WebAssembly runtime. But you generally don’t write programs in Wasm directly.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Don't like a columnist's opinion? Los Angeles Times offers an AI-generated opposing viewpoint
item.description: In a colorful commentary for the Los Angeles Times, Matt K. Lewis argued that callousness is a central feature of the second Trump administration, particularly its policies of deportation and bureaucratic cutbacks.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Meta CEO Zuckerberg considered spinning off Instagram in 2018 over antitrust worries, email says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg once considered separating Instagram from its parent company due to worries about antitrust litigation, according to an email shown Tuesday on the second day of an antitrust trial alleging Meta illegally monopolized the social media market.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: The VA EHR modernization roadmap under the Trump administration
item.description: A key focus will be on modernizing federal technology and software to maximize governmental efficiency and productivity. The post The VA EHR modernization roadmap under the Trump administration first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: China pursuing 3 alleged US operatives over cyberattacks during Asian Games
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — China said Tuesday it is pursuing three alleged U.S. operatives accused of carrying out cyberattacks on Chinese infrastructure during the Asian Games held in the city of Harbin in February. A notice from the Harbin police headquarters named them as Katheryn A. Wilson, Robert J.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER BODEEN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Smarter dairy farms where robots milk the cows
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson discusses how robots can milk, feed and clean cows on dairy farms, boosting efficiency and comfort.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg takes the stand in historic antitrust trial
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg took to the witness stand on the first day of a historic antitrust trial to defend his company against allegations it illegally monopolized the social media market.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: AI isn’t one size fits all as federal agencies seek the right AI tools for mission success
item.description: Federal agencies should consider widening their focus on AI implementations beyond just generative AI. The post AI isn’t one size fits all as federal agencies seek the right AI tools for mission success first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Intel sells majority stake in Altera to Silver Lake investors in a deal valued at $8.75 billion
item.description: Intel announced Monday that it was selling the majority of its Altera chip business to technology investor Silver Lake in a deal valued at $8.75 billion.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Japanese leader says he hopes Osaka expo will help reunite a divided world
item.description: OSAKA, Japan (AP) — Japanese Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba, speaking at Saturday's opening ceremony for the Osaka expo, expressed his hope that the event will help restore global unity in a world plagued by conflicts and trade wars.
item.dc:creator: AYAKA McGILL and MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, ABC 4
